macro.intro: The macroeconomic landscape is characterized by rising inflation with CPI YoY at 330.29, up from 327.46. The 10Y TIPS Real Yield has slightly decreased to 1.92%, indicating a potential shift in investor sentiment. The Fed Funds Rate remains stable at 3.64%, while the 10Y-2Y Yield Spread narrows to 0.5%, suggesting a flattening yield curve. No major economic events are scheduled today, allowing markets to focus on geopolitical tensions and corporate earnings.

setups.name: WTI Crude Oil Recovery Play
setups.rationale: WTI Crude Oil has slipped below $100 due to hopes of US-Iran negotiations. With geopolitical tensions still high, a rebound is likely. The current price of $99.50 offers a strategic entry point, considering the CPI YoY inflation at 330.29, which could drive energy prices higher.

metrics.label: CPI YoY Inflation
metrics.text: High inflation supports energy price increases

metrics.text: No major events today, focus on geopolitical news

metrics.text: Not available, but sentiment suggests risk-on

concerns.text: Further de-escalation in US-Iran tensions could push prices lower

concerns.label: Secondary risk
concerns.text: Unexpected increase in US crude inventories

setups.hedge: Consider shorting EUR/USD if oil prices fall below $97.00

setups.name: S&P 500 Overbought Correction
setups.rationale: The S&P 500 is trading at 4325.00 with an RSI nearing 70, indicating potential overbought conditions. With no major economic events today, the index may face a correction if geopolitical tensions escalate.

concerns.label: Primary risk
concerns.text: Continued bullish sentiment could push index higher

setups.hedge: Consider long VIX if S&P 500 exceeds 4400.00

setups.name: EUR/USD Breakout Play
setups.rationale: EUR/USD is approaching a key resistance at 1.1000, currently trading at 1.0950. With the 10Y-2Y Yield Spread narrowing to 0.5%, a breakout could occur if US economic data remains stable.

metrics.label: Economic event

concerns.text: US dollar strength could cap EUR/USD gains

setups.hedge: Consider shorting S&P 500 if EUR/USD fails to break 1.1000

contrarian.body: Gold has fallen from a one-month peak, currently at 1985.00, as investors anticipate US-Iran negotiations. With CPI YoY inflation at 330.29, a short position could capitalize on potential downside if talks progress.
contrarian.warning: Geopolitical tensions remain high. Unexpected escalation could drive gold prices higher, triggering stops.

avoid.reason: Shares sink due to Iran war impacting luxury revival.
